fault_hypothesis: The overlap condition on line 77 uses strict inequalities (b <= s or a >= e), which allows adjacent/touching windows (where b==s or a==e) to pass through without being flagged as conflicts. The correct condition should use strict inequalities in the other direction: overlap exists when NOT (b < s or a > e), i.e., touching endpoints should count as conflicts.

spec.expr: not any((b__1 == s or a__1 == e) and not any(not (b__1 <= s or a__1 >= e) for s, e in timeline) for s, e in timeline)

constraints.intent: When any existing booking shares an endpoint with the new window, the overlap check should detect it as a conflict; touching windows must not slip through.

spec.expr: all((not (b__1 < s or a__1 > e)) == (not (b__1 <= s or a__1 >= e)) or (b__1 == s or a__1 == e) for s, e in timeline) == False or len(timeline) == 0

constraints.intent: The non-overlap predicate using <= and >= differs from using < and > exactly when endpoints touch; this constraint flags that discrepancy when touching windows exist.

spec.expr: all(b__1 < s or a__1 > e for s, e in timeline)

constraints.intent: After passing the overlap check (else branch), every existing booking must be strictly non-touching with the new window — b must be strictly less than s or a strictly greater than e.

spec.expr: all(b__1 < s or a__1 > e for s, e in timeline)

constraints.intent: result is only computed when no conflict was detected; at that point the new window must be strictly non-adjacent to all existing bookings.
